description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a predominantly gray coloration and subtle brownish streaks running across its surface. The stone features a rough, uneven texture with visible stratification and a prominent natural fracture line, suggesting it may have broken off from a larger rock. One side displays a hand-written marking in red, possibly for identification or cataloging purposes. The surface is mostly matte, with some areas appearing slightly smoother due to natural weathering. The stone's edges are jagged and angular, with no signs of artificial shaping or smoothing. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rough, gray stone with brownish streaks, jagged edges, and a visible fracture line, featuring a hand-written red marking on one side.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, flat surface with a visible fracture line and a red hand-written marking.
description_view_1: This angle shows the stone's more angular, pointed end and emphasizes its rough, unmarked surface.
description_view_2: This perspective reveals the layered structure and brownish streaks running diagonally across the stone.
description_view_3: This view displays the stone's irregular, blocky profile and the transition between its rough and slightly smoother surfaces.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural stratification and veining with irregular brownish streaks and a hand-written red marking
attribute_weathering: Rough with some smoother patches, indicating moderate natural weathering and exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends with natural ground cover or landscaping materials, may contrast with paved surfaces or manicured lawns
